html:
Exclusive to:

The Ancient Lunarune Stones are Structures exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. They can be found in the Ancient Archive biome. Their only purpose is to be examined by the player to reveal a part of the Ancient Civilization history.

Contents

  • 1 Quotes
    • 1.1 First
    • 1.2 Second
    • 1.3 Third
    • 1.4 Fourth
    • 1.5 Fifth
  • 2 Translate
  • 3 Trivia

Quotes

First

Second

Third

Fourth

Fifth

Translate

When wearing Ancient Masks:

  • "We remain faithful to Alter, as Alter remains faithful to us. Ever watching, knowing, waiting."
  • "Alter, Glorious Moon in the cold night sky. Cast your Life-giving Light on us forever."
  • "Mighty Alter, the source of our strength, our sole provider. We are loyal to none but You."
  • "Alter is our Champion. Alter is the Champion. The Champion is Alter."
  • "Alter is good. Alter shall not forsake us. Alter shall return."

Trivia

  • The Ancient Lunarune Stones were introduced in the Forgotten Knowledge update.
  • Only Wickerbottom, Maxwell, and Wortox are able to understand the stones to some extent, and have different quotes for each.
DSTDSRoGSWHam
plainText:
Exclusive to:
Ancient Lunarune Stone
1
2
3
Miscellaneous
"archive_rune_statue"
The Ancient Lunarune Stones are Structures exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. They can be found in the Ancient Archive biome. Their only purpose is to be examined by the player to reveal a part of the Ancient Civilization history.
Contents
1 Quotes
1.1 First
1.2 Second
1.3 Third
1.4 Fourth
1.5 Fifth
2 Translate
3 Trivia
Quotes[edit | edit source]
First[edit | edit source]
“So much knowledge, if only I could read it!”
–Wilson
“A bunch of old mumbo jumbo.”
–Willow
“Is nice statue, but is covered in scribblemarks.”
–Wolfgang
“Words of a dead language.”
–Wendy
“INSUFFICIENT DATA, UNABLE TO TRANSLATE”
–WX-78
“Fascinating! These glyphs seem to be the base that their language evolved from.”
–Wickerbottom
“Now that's a head scratcher.”
–Woodie
“These runes are... different.”
–Maxwell
“These runes are föreign tö me.”
–Wigfrid
“We sure wish we knew what it says!”
–Webber
“I'm afraid its meaning is lost on me.”
–Warly
“Skritch scratches”
–Wormwood
“I can't make heads or tails of it.”
–Winona
“Oh, what's this? A bit of light reading?”
–Wortox
“Glorph... it too hard to read.”
–Wurt
“I sure wish I could read it.”
–Walter
“I won't waste my time trying to decipher this gibberish.”
–Wanda
Second[edit | edit source]
“These markings look different from the ones in the rest of the ruins.”
–Wilson
“If it doesn't burn I don't care.”
–Willow
“Very fancy.”
–Wolfgang
“It was important enough to carve into stone, but we'll never know the meaning.”
–Wendy
“SUCH A CRUDE WAY OF RECORDING DATA”
–WX-78
“The introduction of shadow fuel must have had a tremendous impact on their culture.”
–Wickerbottom
“I never even got a handle on French, don't expect me to read that.”
–Woodie
“I can only glean a hint of their meaning.”
–Maxwell
“Perhaps they speak öf ancient battles.”
–Wigfrid
“It's very pretty.”
–Webber
“So many secrets lost to time.”
–Warly
“Pretty”
–Wormwood
“Ancient writing ain't exactly my specialty.”
–Winona
“It seems they were quite enamoured with our fickle moon once.”
–Wortox
“Someone read story!”
–Wurt
“I wonder what it says.”
–Walter
“I'd go back and ask what it means, but this time period is a bit beyond my reach.”
–Wanda
Third[edit | edit source]
“So much knowledge, if only I could read it!”
–Wilson
“A bunch of old mumbo jumbo.”
–Willow
“Is nice statue, but is covered in scribblemarks.”
–Wolfgang
“What a pity.”
–Wendy
“INSUFFICIENT DATA, UNABLE TO TRANSLATE”
–WX-78
“These glyphs must pre-date their discovery of the shadow fuel.”
–Wickerbottom
“Now that's a head scratcher.”
–Woodie
“This symbol here keeps repeating...”
–Maxwell
“These runes are föreign tö me.”
–Wigfrid
“We sure wish we knew what it says!”
–Webber
“I'm afraid its meaning is lost on me.”
–Warly
“Skritch scratches”
–Wormwood
“I can't make heads or tails of it.”
–Winona
“Now this one doesn't make much sense at all. Unless...”
–Wortox
“Glorph... it too hard to read.”
–Wurt
“I sure wish I could read it.”
–Walter
“I won't waste my time trying to decipher this gibberish.”
–Wanda
Fourth[edit | edit source]
“These markings look different from the ones in the rest of the ruins.”
–Wilson
“If it doesn't burn I don't care.”
–Willow
“Very fancy.”
–Wolfgang
“It was important enough to carve into stone, but we'll never know the meaning.”
–Wendy
“SUCH A CRUDE WAY OF RECORDING DATA”
–WX-78
“This glyph in particular keeps repeating... if only I could decipher its meaning.”
–Wickerbottom
“I never even got a handle on French, don't expect me to read that.”
–Woodie
“"To change?" No. It's a name...”
–Maxwell
“Perhaps they speak öf ancient battles.”
–Wigfrid
“It's very pretty.”
–Webber
“So many secrets lost to time.”
–Warly
“Pretty”
–Wormwood
“Ancient writing ain't exactly my specialty.”
–Winona
“Oh my! My, my my!”
–Wortox
“Someone read story!”
–Wurt
“I wonder what it says.”
–Walter
“I'd go back and ask what it means, but this time period is a bit beyond my reach.”
–Wanda
Fifth[edit | edit source]
“So much knowledge, if only I could read it!”
–Wilson
“A bunch of old mumbo jumbo.”
–Willow
“Is nice statue, but is covered in scribblemarks.”
–Wolfgang
“What a pity.”
–Wendy
“INSUFFICIENT DATA, UNABLE TO TRANSLATE”
–WX-78
“This will need further study.”
–Wickerbottom
“Now that's a head scratcher.”
–Woodie
“"Alter"?”
–Maxwell
“These runes are föreign tö me.”
–Wigfrid
“We sure wish we knew what it says!”
–Webber
“I'm afraid its meaning is lost on me.”
–Warly
“Skritch scratches”
–Wormwood
“I can't make heads or tails of it.”
–Winona
“Hyuyu, I shouldn't spoil the surprise!”
–Wortox
“Glorph... it too hard to read.”
–Wurt
“I sure wish I could read it.”
–Walter
“I won't waste my time trying to decipher this gibberish.”
–Wanda
Translate[edit | edit source]
When wearing Ancient Masks:
"We remain faithful to Alter, as Alter remains faithful to us. Ever watching, knowing, waiting."
"Alter, Glorious Moon in the cold night sky. Cast your Life-giving Light on us forever."
"Mighty Alter, the source of our strength, our sole provider. We are loyal to none but You."
"Alter is our Champion. Alter is the Champion. The Champion is Alter."
"Alter is good. Alter shall not forsake us. Alter shall return."
Trivia[edit | edit source]
The Ancient Lunarune Stones were introduced in the Forgotten Knowledge update.
Only Wickerbottom, Maxwell, and Wortox are able to understand the stones to some extent, and have different quotes for each.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Đá Lunarune Cổ là Kiến trúc độc quyền của Don't Starve Together, được giới thiệu trong Return of Them. Chúng có thể được tìm thấy trong quần thể Kho Lưu trữ Cổ đại. Mục đích duy nhất của chúng là để người chơi kiểm tra để tiết lộ một phần lịch sử của Nền văn minh cổ đại.
title: Ancient Lunarune Stone

}}
The '''Ancient Lunarune Stones''' are [[Structures]] exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. They can be found in the [[Ancient Archive]] biome. Their only purpose is to be examined by the player to reveal a part of the [[Ancient Civilization]] history.

=={{Pic|32|Elytra's Mask}} Translate==
When wearing [[Ancient Mask]]s:
* "We remain faithful to Alter, as Alter remains faithful to us. Ever watching, knowing, waiting."
* "Alter, Glorious Moon in the cold night sky. Cast your Life-giving Light on us forever."
* "Mighty Alter, the source of our strength, our sole provider. We are loyal to none but You."
* "Alter is our Champion. Alter is the Champion. The Champion is Alter."
* "Alter is good. Alter shall not forsake us. Alter shall return."

== {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* The Ancient Lunarune Stones were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Forgotten Knowledge|Forgotten Knowledge]]'' update.
* Only Wickerbottom, Maxwell, and Wortox are able to understand the stones to some extent, and have different quotes for each.
